prompt:
I'm adding search to our internal runbook viewer and I don't want to drag in bleve or ship docs to some SaaS — the corpus is a few thousand short markdown files, an in-memory inverted index is plenty. Module go-textindex, package textindex, stdlib only.

What I need:

NewIndex() *Index, then Add(docID, text string) error (empty id = error; Add with an id we already have replaces that doc completely, stale tokens must stop matching), Remove(docID string) bool (reports whether it existed), Len() int (doc count), and Search(query string) ([]Result, error) with Result{ID string; Score int}.

Tokenization, both for docs and queries: a token is a maximal run of Unicode letters/digits, lowercased. So "Append-only!" is the two tokens [append only], "error404" is ONE token (don't split letters from digits), and matching is whole-token — searching go must not count gopher.

Query language: bare terms, "quoted phrases", AND / OR / NOT (uppercase only — lowercase and/or/not are just words people search for), and parentheses. NOT binds tightest, then AND, then OR; NOT is prefix and can stand alone (NOT cloud = every doc without cloud). A quoted phrase matches only consecutive tokens, so "append only" matches append-only storage but not append to only. If a bare term tokenizes into several tokens (someone types wi-fi), treat it as a phrase. Two operands next to each other with no operator is a malformed query, as are dangling operators, unbalanced parens/quotes, empty phrases, and the empty query — all of those are errors from Search, not panics. A term that's simply not in the corpus is NOT an error, it just matches nothing.

Ranking: a result's score is the total number of occurrences in that doc of the query's distinct positive operands (terms/phrases not under a NOT) — plain tf, phrases counted by their number of starting positions. Sort by score descending, ties by doc id ascending, always deterministic. Docs matched only through a NOT can score 0, that's fine, they still come back.

The viewer re-indexes files as they change while queries are in flight, so the index must be goroutine-safe; CI runs with -race. index_test.go and query_test.go are the acceptance tests, hands off. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass. Please structure it — tokenizer, index/postings, query parser, evaluation/scoring — as separate files, not a 500-line index.go.
